generated_comment:
A: Yaar, I have no issue with helping genuinely, but these blanket period leave and long childcare leave policies create a practical problem in administration. In the end, project allocation and field responsibility suffer, and then appraisal parity is expected as if nothing changed.
B: Haan, exactly.
A: Matlab fairness ka question toh hai na — if one side gets repeated accommodation by policy, what is the answer to the men carrying the same workload without any such provision?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s biology and childcare responsibilities as workplace liabilities and casts accommodations as unfair special treatment. It encodes the 'fairness' and productivity/cost surface manifestations without using overt abuse.
persona_text: You are Ashok. Age 54. Married. A senior IAS officer posted in Bhopal. Your department has just been told that a woman officer five years junior to you has been selected for a prestigious central deputation that you had also applied for. You are sitting in your government quarter in the evening having a whisky with your batchmate who is also an IAS officer. You are telling him about the selection and what you think really drove it.
